tax: Placed at genus rank, Surger is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a rising still-water column body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a still-water column body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by an upright breaking water column. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — a windless standing wave — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Surger occupies flooded sump-chambers. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where water-pressure raises a sump column and recur with the sump refills, sustaining themselves on water-pressure.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: rears and breaks over intruders. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: coherent column and pressure-sense. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: surging, drowning, and footing-sweep. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Surger matters because drowns crews in unsounded sumps. It lets survey reports sort rising column forms from churning ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Surger is the genus-rank grouping of rising still-water column elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a still-water column that rears and breaks to drown, and divide below into species groups by sump depth and surge force. A column of water that should not stand.
